title: coap_msg.c: encode_inbox_cbor bounds check integer underflow
description:
File: lichen/subsys/lichen/coap/coap_msg.c
Line: 650

The bounds check in encode_inbox_cbor() uses:
  for (size_t i = 0; i < count && off < buf_size - 100; i++)

If buf_size < 100, the expression 'buf_size - 100' underflows (size_t is unsigned), wrapping to a very large value (~UINT64_MAX). This makes the condition 'off < large_number' always true, effectively disabling bounds checking.

The loop then writes to buf[] without proper protection, causing buffer overflow.

Same pattern exists in coap_keys.c encode_keys_list_cbor() at line 679, which is already tracked by project-LICHEN-byge.

Fix: Use safe comparison that doesn't underflow:
  for (size_t i = 0; i < count && off + 100 < buf_size; i++)

Or add explicit check:
  if (buf_size < 100) return 0;

Impact: Buffer overflow when encode_inbox_cbor is called with small buffer (theoretical since callers currently use 512-byte buffers).
